note_text:
AUTHOR: Samih Qudah STATUS: COMPLETED
A 64 year old gentleman, a case of metastatic NSCLC, on carboplatin/pemetrexed/pembrolizumab, cycle 1 was held today.
Seen because of cough, wt loss and pleuritic pain; symptoms were present for 6 weeks before immunotherapy decision.
- CT chest 07/03/2026: bulky RUL mass, lymphangitic spread, small pleural effusion; no radiology comment of pneumonitis.
- Labs: Hb 10.2 WBC 12.1 ANC 9.8 Plt 410 Cr 0.9 Na 132, TSH baseline 4.1.
- Meds: codeine syrup for cough, paracetamol for pain, ceftriaxone started yesterday for post-obstructive pneumonia.
No diarrhea, no rash, no new SOB after pembrolizumab because treatment not yet given.
Exam: cachectic, decreased air entry Rt upper zone, abdomen soft, PS 2.
plan
admit for antibiotics and drainage if effusion increases, start systemic therapy after infection controlled.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Hiba Nabulsi STATUS: COMPLETED
A 46 year old lady, a case of ovarian ca with peritoneal/omental mets, on weekly paclitaxel planned, not given for 3 weeks.
C/o abdominal bloating, early satiety, mild nausea and low Na 127; family worried it is from chemo but last paclitaxel was remote and sx progressed with ascites.
- US 07/05/2026: large malignant ascites, no hydronephrosis.
- Labs: Na 127, Cr 0.8, Alb 2.7, Hb 9.5, WBC 6.2, Plt 280.
- Meds: spironolactone started today for ascites, metoclopramide PRN for nausea, oxycodone PRN for pain.
No tingling, no infusion rash, no mouth ulcers, no bleeding.
Exam: distended abdomen with shifting dullness, soft, no peritonitis, PS 2.
plan
therapeutic paracentesis, salt/fluid advice, delay paclitaxel until symptoms improve.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Karim AlQaisi STATUS: COMPLETED
A 57 year old gentleman, a case of rectal ca with liver mets, on FOLFIRI + bevacizumab, cycle 5 on 07/01/2026.
He reports 7 watery stools/day starting 24 hr after irinotecan; had baseline 1-2 loose stools from rectal mass but this is clearly worse.
- Stool C diff negative, culture pending; no travel, no sick contact.
- Labs: K 3.2, Cr 1.0, WBC 4.9 ANC 3.0, Plt 188.
- Also has mild rectal bleeding from tumor before bevacizumab, unchanged.
Exam: mild dehydration, abdomen soft no guarding, PR small tumor ooze.
Assessment: irinotecan delayed diarrhea; rectal bleeding disease related.
plan
loperamide high dose, IV fluids, reduce irinotecan next cycle, continue bev if bleeding stable.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Manar Zreiqat STATUS: COMPLETED
A 39 year old lady, a case of classical Hodgkin lymphoma, on AVD after bleomycin stopped previously, cycle 4 on 07/02/2026.
She came with palpitations and anxiety before chemo, ECG before infusion normal; later QTc 512 after ondansetron IV plus home escitalopram.
- K 3.9 Mg 1.9 Ca 9.0, TSH 2.1, troponin negative.
- No syncope, no chest pain; old bleomycin cough resolved months ago.
Exam: stable, HR 104, chest clear, abdomen soft.
Assessment: drug related QT prolongation likely ondansetron/escitalopram combination; anxiety pre-existing.
plan
avoid ondansetron, use aprepitant/low dose olanzapine with ECG monitoring, discuss SSRI with psych.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Zeina Salameh STATUS: COMPLETED
A 71 year old gentleman, a case of metastatic prostate ca, on abiraterone + prednisone, leuprolide, and denosumab.
Seen for fatigue, edema and low K. Edema preceded abiraterone with hypoalbumin; K 2.8 developed after abiraterone dose increase despite supplements.
- Labs: K 2.8, Na 139, Cr 1.0, ALT 28, albumin 2.5; urine protein negative.
- CT 07/04/2026: stable bone mets, new small ascites likely liver disease.
No diarrhea, no vomiting, no adrenal crisis sx.
Exam: bilateral ankle edema, abdomen soft mild ascites, BP 142/88.
Assessment: abiraterone mineralocorticoid effect causing hypokalemia; edema multifactorial not clearly drug related.
plan
hold abiraterone 1 week, increase prednisone compliance, K replacement, review albumin/ascites.

suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Hypokalemia developed after abiraterone dose increase and is assessed as mineralocorticoid effect from abiraterone.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Rula Bseiso STATUS: COMPLETED
A 50 year old lady, a case of metastatic melanoma, on pembrolizumab, cycle 7 on 06/27/2026.
She has chronic IBS diarrhea 2/day for years, but now 9/day watery stools after pembrolizumab with crampy pain; also TSH 0.02 with tremor after last two cycles.
- Stool PCR negative, calprotectin high; CT abdomen no obstruction.
- Labs: FT4 2.4, ALT 44, Cr 0.8, K 3.4.
- No fever, no laxative use, no blood in stool.
Exam: mild diffuse tenderness, no guarding, pulse 112.
Assessment: immune colitis and immune thyroiditis; baseline IBS noted but not enough to explain change.
plan
hold pembro, prednisone, endocrine review, beta blocker, repeat TFT.

A 60 year old gentleman, a case of pancreatic ca, on modified FOLFIRINOX, cycle 3 on 07/01/2026.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Mays AbuFares STATUS: COMPLETED
A 42 year old lady, a case of TNBC, on pembrolizumab + carboplatin/paclitaxel, cycle 6 on 07/03/2026.
Complains of fatigue, numb toes, cough, and new LFT rise. Fatigue existed with anemia before treatment.
Numb toes became constant after weekly paclitaxel; ALT/AST 360/310 after pembrolizumab with viral screen negative; cough is productive with RLL infiltrate and fever.
- Labs: Hb 8.7, ANC 2.2, Plt 160, bili 0.8, TSH 3.2.
- CXR: RLL infiltrate; no bilateral GGO. No diarrhea, no rash.
Exam: mild sensory loss toes, chest RLL creps, abdomen soft.
Assessment: paclitaxel neuropathy, immune hepatitis likely, bacterial pneumonia not ICI pneumonitis.
plan
hold pembro/paclitaxel, start prednisone after hepatology review, ceftriaxone for pneumonia, pregabalin.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: Numb toes became constant after weekly paclitaxel
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Toe numbness became constant after weekly paclitaxel and is assessed as paclitaxel neuropathy.
Transaminases rose after pembrolizumab with negative viral screen and are assessed as immune hepatitis.
Exam confirms sensory loss in the same distribution as the paclitaxel-linked numb toes.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Nadine Safadi STATUS: COMPLETED
A 69 year old gentleman, a case of metastatic RCC, on cabozantinib 60 mg, started 5 weeks ago, with warfarin for mechanical valve.
Seen for mouth pain, BP, diarrhea and anemia. Oral ulcers began after cabozantinib and improved on 3 day hold; BP 178/96 since cabozantinib; INR 5.6 after starting fluconazole for thrush while on warfarin.
Diarrhea was present from pancreatic insufficiency before cabozantinib and unchanged on enzymes.
- Labs: Hb 9.0, Cr 1.0, AST 51, urine protein 1+.
No melena, no hematemesis, no fever.
Assessment: cabozantinib mucositis and hypertension; warfarin-fluconazole interaction causing supratherapeutic INR; diarrhea not drug related.
plan
reduce cabozantinib, amlodipine, stop fluconazole, adjust warfarin, oral care.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: Oral ulcers began after cabozantinib and improved on 3 day hold
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Oral ulcers began after cabozantinib and improved with drug hold, and the assessment calls it cabozantinib mucositis.
The elevated blood pressure occurred since cabozantinib and is assessed as cabozantinib hypertension.

suspected_adr_signals.drug_class: vitamin K antagonist and azole antifungal

AUTHOR: Omar Jaradat STATUS: COMPLETED
A 74 year old gentleman, a case of metastatic urothelial ca, on enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab, cycle 3 on 07/06/2026.
He has dysuria from tumor invasion unchanged, but new painful diffuse blistering rash after enfortumab, glucose 290 after dexamethasone premed, and dry eyes/blurred vision after enfortumab.
- Ophtho: keratitis, no retinal mets.
- Labs: Cr 1.2, Hb 10.4, ANC 2.8, ALT 40, HbA1c old 5.6.
- No mucosal sloughing, no fever, no diarrhea.

plan
hold enfortumab, dermatology, lubricating drops, insulin PRN, continue pembrolizumab only after rash improves.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Faris Mdanat STATUS: COMPLETED
A 33 year old gentleman, a case of germ cell tumor, on BEP, cycle 2 day 15 on 07/07/2026.
Complaints are mixed: cancer back pain unchanged, anxiety-related chest tightness before chemo, but Cr 1.9 after cisplatin, Mg 0.8 after cisplatin, tinnitus after cisplatin, and dry cough/SOB with DLCO drop after bleomycin.
- CTA: no PE, no infection consolidation; HRCT subtle basal interstitial opacity.
- Labs: Cr baseline 0.8 now 1.9, Mg 0.8, Hb 11.9, WBC 3.2.
- no fever, no sputum, no hematuria.
Exam: fine basal crackles, abdomen soft, PS 1.
Assessment: cisplatin nephrotoxicity, cisplatin hypomagnesemia, cisplatin ototoxicity, probable bleomycin pneumonitis.
plan
stop bleomycin, hold cisplatin today, IV Mg/hydration, audiogram, repeat PFT.

suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Creatinine rose after cisplatin and is assessed as cisplatin nephrotoxicity.
Low magnesium occurred after cisplatin and is assessed as cisplatin hypomagnesemia.
Tinnitus occurred after cisplatin and is assessed as cisplatin ototoxicity.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: dry cough/SOB with DLCO drop after bleomycin
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Dry cough and SOB with DLCO drop occurred after bleomycin and are assessed as probable bleomycin pneumonitis.

note_text:
STATUS POST:
metastatic colon ca on CAPOX + bevacizumab, taking warfarin for AF, recently given clarithromycin for sinusitis
CHIEF COMPLAIN:
came to er for hand pain, diarrhea, high INR and headache
painful palms/soles with peeling after capecitabine; watery diarrhea 6/day after capecitabine, C diff neg; INR 6.4 after clarithromycin started while on warfarin; BP 184/106 since bevacizumab
headache likely from BP, no focal weakness, no GI bleeding
VITAL SIGNS:
 T: 98.4 F (36.9 C)
 P: 92
 R: 18
 B/P: 184/106
 Pulse Oximetry: 98%
PAIN SCORE: 7/10
PHYSICAL EXAM:
 PT CONSCIOUS, ORIENTED
 PALMAR PLANTAR PEELING, ABD SOFT
 NEURO GROSSLY INTACT
ASSESSMENT AND CONCLUSION:
capecitabine HFS + diarrhea, warfarin-clarithromycin interaction with high INR, bevacizumab hypertension
PLAN OF CARE:
hold capecitabine and bev, stop clarithromycin, vitamin K low dose, loperamide, topical urea
/es/ Khaled Shannak, MD
Signed: 07/14/2026 16:12

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: painful palms/soles with peeling after capecitabine
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Palmar-plantar peeling occurred after capecitabine and is assessed as capecitabine HFS.
Watery diarrhea followed capecitabine and is assessed as capecitabine diarrhea.

suspected_adr_signals.drug_class: vitamin K antagonist and macrolide antibiotic
suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: INR 6.4 after clarithromycin started while on warfarin
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: INR rose after clarithromycin was started while on warfarin and the assessment states interaction.
The blood pressure elevation occurred since bevacizumab and is assessed as bevacizumab hypertension.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Dana Shomali STATUS: COMPLETED
A 66 year old lady, a case of metastatic breast ca, on ribociclib + letrozole, plus ondansetron PRN and levofloxacin recently for UTI.
Review today confusing because she had palpitations before treatment, but ECG now QTc 530 after ribociclib with levofloxacin/ondansetron; ANC 0.8 after ribociclib; AST/ALT 210/188 after ribociclib; nausea improved with ondansetron.
- Labs: K 4.0 Mg 2.0, bili 0.6, Cr 0.9.
- UTI symptoms resolved, urine culture sterile.
No syncope, no fever, no jaundice.
Exam stable, no abdominal tenderness, PS 0.
Assessment: ribociclib neutropenia and transaminitis, QT prolongation likely combined QT drugs; nausea not ADR today.
plan
hold ribociclib, stop levofloxacin/avoid ondansetron, repeat ECG/LFT/CBC in 1 week.

suspected_adr_signals.drug_class: CDK4/6 inhibitor with fluoroquinolone antibiotic and 5-HT3 antagonist
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: QTc prolongation occurred after combined QT-prolonging drugs and is assessed as likely combined QT drugs.
Low ANC followed ribociclib and is assessed as ribociclib neutropenia.
Transaminases rose after ribociclib and are assessed as ribociclib transaminitis.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: QT prolongation likely combined QT drugs
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: The assessment attributes QT prolongation to the combined QT drugs.

note_text:
STATUS POST:
relapsed AML on venetoclax/azacitidine, posaconazole prophylaxis added, also linezolid for VRE and sertraline home med
CHIEF COMPLAIN:
came to er for severe weakness, bruising, tremor/clonus, high ven level suspected
ANC 0.2 after venetoclax/azacitidine; Plt 22 after azacitidine but also linezolid may contribute; profound fatigue and mucositis worsened after posaconazole was added with venetoclax not dose reduced; tremor/clonus after linezolid + sertraline
no active bleeding, no seizure, no fever today
VITAL SIGNS:
 T: 98.9 F (37.2 C)
 P: 112
 R: 20
 B/P: 118/70
 Pulse Oximetry: 97%
PAIN SCORE: 4/10
PHYSICAL EXAM:
 PT CONSCIOUS, ANXIOUS
 ORAL ULCERS, ECCHYMOSES ARMS
 CLONUS BOTH FEET, ABD SOFT
ASSESSMENT AND CONCLUSION:
ven/aza myelosuppression with neutropenia and thrombocytopenia, venetoclax-posaconazole excess toxicity causing mucositis/fatigue, serotonin syndrome from linezolid/sertraline
PLAN OF CARE:
hold venetoclax/aza/linezolid, adjust posa interaction, platelet transfusion if bleeding, supportive mouth care
/es/ Reem AlKarmi, MD
Signed: 07/15/2026 06:07

suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Low ANC occurred after venetoclax/azacitidine and is assessed as ven/aza myelosuppression.
Thrombocytopenia occurred after azacitidine and is included in ven/aza myelosuppression.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: mucositis worsened after posaconazole was added with venetoclax not dose reduced
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Mucositis worsened after posaconazole was added without venetoclax dose reduction and is assessed as excess toxicity.
Tremor and clonus occurred after linezolid plus sertraline and are assessed as serotonin syndrome.

unlinked_adverse_events.evidence_quote: came to er for severe weakness, bruising, tremor/clonus

note_text:
AUTHOR: Leila Haddadin STATUS: COMPLETED
A 58 year old lady, a case of metastatic gastric ca, on nivolumab + FOLFOX, cycle 9 on 07/04/2026, with apixaban for old PE.
Hard to separate disease sx: appetite loss and epigastric pain predate treatment; ascites is malignant. However she developed watery diarrhea 10/day after nivolumab, TSH 24 with fatigue after nivolumab, numb/cold painful fingers after oxaliplatin, Plt 52 after oxaliplatin/5FU, and melena while on apixaban.
- Stool PCR negative, CT no colitis perforation, Hb 8.8 from 10.5, Cr 0.9.
- no fever, no hematemesis, no bowel obstruction.
Exam: mild dehydration, fingertip dysesthesia, abdomen distended but soft.
Assessment: immune colitis + immune hypothyroidism, oxaliplatin neuropathy, chemo thrombocytopenia, apixaban GI bleed.
plan
hold nivo/chemo/apixaban, prednisone, levothyroxine, IV PPI, platelet monitoring, loperamide only after infection ruled out.

suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Watery diarrhea occurred after nivolumab and is assessed as immune colitis.
Elevated TSH with fatigue occurred after nivolumab and is assessed as immune hypothyroidism.
Cold painful numb fingers occurred after oxaliplatin and are assessed as oxaliplatin neuropathy.
Platelets dropped after oxaliplatin/5FU and are assessed as chemo thrombocytopenia.
Melena occurred while on apixaban and is assessed as apixaban GI bleed.

unlinked_adverse_events.evidence_quote: appetite loss and epigastric pain predate treatment

note_text:
STATUS POST:
AML consolidation, on vancomycin + cefepime for FN, posaconazole prophylaxis, oxycodone PRN, metoclopramide scheduled
CHIEF COMPLAIN:
came to er for AKI, confusion/movements, rash, constipation
Cr 2.6 after vancomycin trough 31; red flushing and pruritus during vancomycin infusion yesterday; acute dystonia/oculogyric crisis after metoclopramide; diffuse maculopapular rash after cefepime; severe constipation after oxycodone escalation
confusion partly sleep deprivation but dystonia seen; no hypotension, no mucosal lesions, no diarrhea
VITAL SIGNS:
 T: 99.1 F (37.3 C)
 P: 104
 R: 18
 B/P: 116/72
 Pulse Oximetry: 97%
PAIN SCORE: 5/10
PHYSICAL EXAM:
 PT CONSCIOUS, INTERMITTENT EYE DEVIATION
 TRUNK MORBILLIFORM RASH
 ABD DISTENDED, BOWEL SOUNDS SLOW
ASSESSMENT AND CONCLUSION:
vancomycin nephrotoxicity + infusion reaction, metoclopramide EPS, cefepime drug rash, opioid constipation
PLAN OF CARE:
hold vanco/cefepime/metoclopramide, benztropine, bowel regimen, renal dosing, switch abx
/es/ Dima Kanaan, MD
Signed: 07/16/2026 09:59

suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Creatinine rose after a high vancomycin trough and is assessed as vancomycin nephrotoxicity.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: red flushing and pruritus during vancomycin infusion yesterday
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Flushing and pruritus occurred during vancomycin infusion and are assessed as an infusion reaction.
Dystonia and oculogyric crisis occurred after metoclopramide and are assessed as metoclopramide EPS.
Diffuse maculopapular rash occurred after cefepime and is assessed as cefepime drug rash.
Severe constipation followed oxycodone escalation and is assessed as opioid constipation.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Ziad Tannous STATUS: COMPLETED
A 70 year old gentleman, a case of metastatic prostate ca, on docetaxel + prednisone, denosumab monthly, amiodarone, and recent levofloxacin for bronchitis.
No cancer progression on CT. Today: leg edema after docetaxel despite stable albumin, glucose 402 after prednisone, Ca 6.8 after denosumab, QTc 540 after levofloxacin added to amiodarone, and black stool after naproxen self-medication for bone pain.
- Hb 8.9 from 10.9, Mg 1.7, K 4.1, Cr 1.0, albumin 3.8.
- cough improved on antibiotic; no syncope, no hematemesis, no diarrhea.
Exam: edema to knees, mild epigastric tenderness, no focal neuro deficit.
Assessment: docetaxel fluid retention, steroid hyperglycemia, denosumab hypocalcemia, QT drug interaction, NSAID related upper GI bleed.
plan
hold docetaxel, insulin, calcium/vit D, stop levofloxacin and naproxen, IV PPI, ECG monitoring.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: leg edema after docetaxel despite stable albumin
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Leg edema occurred after docetaxel despite stable albumin and is assessed as docetaxel fluid retention.
Glucose 402 occurred after prednisone and is assessed as steroid hyperglycemia.
Calcium was low after denosumab and is assessed as denosumab hypocalcemia.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: QTc 540 after levofloxacin added to amiodarone
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: QTc prolongation occurred after levofloxacin was added to amiodarone and is assessed as a QT drug interaction.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: black stool after naproxen self-medication for bone pain
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Black stool occurred after naproxen self-medication and is assessed as NSAID related upper GI bleed.

note_text:
AUTHOR: Sawsan Batarseh STATUS: COMPLETED
A 63 year old lady, a case of metastatic NSCLC EGFR exon19, on osimertinib; admitted last week received azithromycin, enoxaparin prophylaxis, dexamethasone for brain edema, and nystatin started later.
Current review: diarrhea after osimertinib is 5/day and worse than baseline IBS; acneiform rash after osimertinib; QTc 522 after osimertinib plus azithromycin; injection site hematoma after enoxaparin; oral candidiasis after dexamethasone course.
- Brain mets edema causes baseline headache, unchanged; CT head no bleed.
- Labs: K 4.3 Mg 2.1 Cr 0.8 ANC 4.0 Plt 190, glucose 210 not treated today.
- no syncope, no fever, no mucosal bleeding.
Exam: mild facial papules, white oral plaques, bruised abdomen injection site, abdomen soft.
Assessment: osimertinib diarrhea/rash, QT interaction with azithro, LMWH hematoma, steroid thrush; headache due brain mets.
plan
continue osimertinib with dose review, stop azithro, ECG in 48h, nystatin, rotate injection site, avoid loperamide until stool test if fever.

suspected_adr_signals.evidence_quote: diarrhea after osimertinib is 5/day and worse than baseline IBS
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: Diarrhea occurred after osimertinib and is worse than baseline IBS, and is assessed as osimertinib diarrhea.
Acneiform rash occurred after osimertinib and is assessed as osimertinib rash.

suspected_adr_signals.drug_class: EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor and macrolide antibiotic
suspected_adr_signals.reasoning_brief: QTc prolongation occurred after osimertinib plus azithromycin and is assessed as a QT interaction.
Injection site hematoma occurred after enoxaparin and is assessed as LMWH hematoma.
Oral candidiasis occurred after dexamethasone course and is assessed as steroid thrush.

unlinked_adverse_events.evidence_quote: Brain mets edema causes baseline headache
